mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.02 in positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rate-hike months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal shows zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) across 2010-2023.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad demand contraction, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index drop below 99.0 during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side stress that triggers short-term equity sell-offs over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances during tightening periods, or the signal loses significance when FEDFUNDS slope is positive.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with rapid interest rate hikes creates a stagflationary shock that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields a negative Rank IC, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation tightening months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate recession fears that force rapid portfolio deleveraging, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of contraction quarters, or the signal shows no statistical edge (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index reading above 101.0 during a post-shock recovery phase confirms supply chain normalization, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered instances during recovery phases, or Rank IC is consistently negative across 2010-2023.
